title: Cluster-Level RBAC
description:
## Cluster Roles and Bindings

Some resources, like Nodes, are cluster-scoped and cannot be accessed using normal Roles and RoleBindings.

**Your task:**

1. Create a ServiceAccount named `monitor-sa` in the `monitoring` namespace.
2. Create a ClusterRole named `node-viewer` that grants `get`, `list`, and `watch` permissions on `nodes`.
3. Create a ClusterRoleBinding named `monitor-node-binding` to bind the ClusterRole to the ServiceAccount.

```bash
# Verify your permissions:
kubectl auth can-i list nodes --as=system:serviceaccount:monitoring:monitor-sa
```

hints.title: Imperative Commands
hints.body: Create the SA, then the ClusterRole, then the ClusterRoleBinding.
hints.command: kubectl create sa monitor-sa -n monitoring && kubectl create clusterrole node-viewer --verb=get,list,watch --resource=nodes && kubectl create clusterrolebinding monitor-node-binding --clusterrole=node-viewer --serviceaccount=monitoring:monitor-sa

setup_commands.command: kubectl create namespace monitoring --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -

commands.command: kubectl get sa monitor-sa -n monitoring -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'
